<think>
The user wants a short, polite two-paragraph email to opposing counsel proposing a one-week extension on a contract-review deadline. Generic wording, no specific agreement references. This is a simple drafting task — no need to search documents or use todos. Just write the email.
</think>

Subject: Request for Brief Extension on Contract Review Deadline

Dear [Opposing Counsel's Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to respectfully request a one-week extension of our current deadline for the contract review. A small additional amount of time would allow us to give the matter the thorough attention it deserves and to ensure that any comments or proposed revisions we share are well-considered and complete.

Please let me know at your earliest convenience whether this adjustment works for you and your client. I am happy to discuss the proposed new timeline or any concerns you may have, and I appreciate your understanding and flexibility. Thank you in advance for your consideration.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Title / Firm]
